title: Im not too sure but
wikitext:
[]: I'm not too sure but             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:05 am Perhaps some of you have thought of it before, but have you ever considered Yui Ikari as the true mastermind behind Evangelion? -Well, one should consider the possibility that she faked her death. Plus, I think she wrote some thesis that really surprised Seele, sorry for the lack of details, I'm just pondering about it in my head. Anyway, I'm not really sure about her intentions. I guess I really should rewatch that certain episode. I'd appreciate your discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:16 am Yui didn't FAKE her death. Yui DID, however, plan to get into the Eva as a means to escape Seele.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:17 am relevant episode is 21' pretty much you're right, except she never died. Remember Akagi's line from 23', "You were there at the exact moment your mother was taken into unit 01." ie, not dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:19 am Are you new to the series? I'm just wondering, because even though we've been over this a ton of times on these forums (please post links others who aren't me) that's pretty good insight if you haven't read any of this stuff before. I highly recommend checking out the ANF graveyard and use the search function and you can read up on a lot of this. Especially check out our Wiki (you can find the link on EvaGeeks home page).

[]: Re: I'm not too sure but             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:23 am bap_120790 wrote: Perhaps some of you have thought of it before, but have you ever considered Yui Ikari as the true mastermind behind Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:25 am Yui-sama obviously didn't want Gendo to start 3I, cause Shogouki kinda...ate Gendo in EoE when 3I was initiated by Gendo...

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:29 am Shiro wrote: Yui-sama obviously didn't want Gendo to start 3I, cause Shogouki kinda...ate Gendo in EoE when 3I was initiated by Gendo...

[]: Re: I'm not too sure but             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:36 am BrikHaus wrote: but I wouldn't go so far as to say she mastermined everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:42 am What I'd say was that she was a few steps ahead of both Seele and Gendo. She was perhaps worried about being offed by Seele ("it seems killing people couldn't be easier"), and wanted to interfere in their plans. Also, she states she was doing what she was doing for Shinji. So, assuming that 3I was inevitable in some form (Angels win, screwed, Angels lose, and Seele does it) then if she 'escapes' inside Sho, She knows Shinji will have the final choice. And I've always likened those "psychological" scenes with Shinji inside Sho as her reaching out to him and helping him to decide what future he wants.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:48 am i think a few of us have made it very clear that, it was Yuis fault everything

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 18, 2007 4:22 am http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=2634 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=3164 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=703

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Nov 18, 2007 4:29 am Sachiel_13 wrote: i think a few of us have made it very clear that, it was Yuis fault everything

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:01 am someone needs to tune up their sarcasm detector...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:07 am

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:37 am One macro per thread is more than enough. TOPIC, bitches.

[]:             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Sun Nov 18, 2007 7:55 am Are you new to the series? I'm just wondering, because even though we've been over this a ton of times on these forums (please post links others who aren't me) that's pretty good insight if you haven't read any of this stuff before. I highly recommend checking out the ANF graveyard and use the search function and you can read up on a lot of this. Especially check out our Wiki (you can find the link on EvaGeeks home page).

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 18, 2007 11:29 am Shiro wrote: Shogouki kinda...ate Gendo in EoE when 3I was initiated by Gendo...

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:48 pm Yui 'dies'-->Gendo upset-->Seele interested in Instrumentality put forward by that bastard Gendo-->Eventually, Seele version defeated-->Gendo version defeated--> Yui's wishes partially fulfilled I think it is more to do with Gendo's emotions after Yui apparantely 'dies'

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sun Nov 18, 2007 6:56 pm NAveryW wrote: If I had seen the series when I was seven (or eleven), it would have probably interested me somewhat, but scared the crap out of me. I mean, EoE? I wouldn't have been able to sleep for days.

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Sun Nov 18, 2007 9:20 pm I agree that Yui was incredibly intelligent and masterminded quite a lot of what happened but unless she was clairvoyant, there was a lot that she could never have planned for. She probably could have figured out how Gendo would act after she entered Eva, but I doubt she could have anticipated his reactions with Shinji or whatever the hell was going on with Rei, not to mention all of the other truly crazy things that happened. Despite this, yes, I believe what eventually happened went according to what Yui planned and that her main goal was to let her son choose the final ending (though if Rei had been Lilith then that might not have happened so I guess...Way to go Gendo!).

Plus, I think she wrote some thesis that really surprised Seele, sorry for the lack of details, I'm just pondering about it in my head. Anyway, I'm no
